subject: Anthropology
title: Language as a System of Symbols
description: A short lesson to help you apply Language as a System of Symbols.

hook.text: The word dog has no bark, no fur, no tail. Yet everyone who hears it pictures a four-legged animal.

buildup.text: Language is a symbolic system. Words are arbitrary signs that carry meaning only because a community agrees.

discovery.text: Ferdinand de Saussure called this the arbitrary nature of the sign — no natural link between sound and meaning.

twist.text: Because symbols are arbitrary, languages can create unlimited meanings. Any sound can mean anything if people agree.

climax.text: Symbols free humans from the concrete present. With words, we can discuss the past, future, and the imaginary.

punchline.text: Words are agreements that unlock infinite meaning.

quiz.question: What does the arbitrary nature of the sign mean?
options: Words sound like what they describe
No natural link exists between a word and its meaning
All languages use the same words
